activities_projects.title: Extended Family Pedigree Analysis
steps: Collect trait data (e.g., attached earlobes or color blindness if known) for 3 generations
Draw detailed pedigree chart using standard symbols
Determine mode of inheritance (autosomal dominant/recessive or sex-linked)
Justify with reasons
activities_projects.evaluation: Chart accuracy, inference, and justification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dihybrid Cross Simulation with Beads
steps: Simulate dihybrid cross (e.g., round/yellow × wrinkled/green peas)
Perform random mating for F2 using beads
Calculate observed phenotypic ratio and compare to 9:3:3:1
Discuss chi-square if deviation
activities_projects.evaluation: Ratio calculation, comparison, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Blood Group Inheritance Case Studies
steps: Take real/fictional parent blood groups (e.g., AB × O)
Predict possible child blood groups with Punnett squares
Research and explain rare cases (e.g., Bombay phenotype impact)
Discuss transfusion implications
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, research depth, and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sex-Linked Traits Probability Calculation
steps: Take color blindness (X-linked recessive) example
If mother carrier × normal father, calculate probabilities for sons/daughters
Extend to multiple generations or hemophilia
Present as report with diagrams

activities_projects.title: Variation Data Collection and Graphing
steps: Measure height or finger length in 40+ students
Plot histogram and calculate mean/range
Discuss causes (genes + environment) and evolutionary significance
Suggest how natural selection acts on it
activities_projects.evaluation: Data quality, graph, and discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mendel's Laws Experimental Design
steps: Design test cross to verify dominance or linkage
Explain controls, variables, sample size
Predict results and how to analyze
Relate to real pea experiments

activities_projects.title: Debate: Genetics vs Environment in Traits
steps: Divide into teams
Use examples (height, intelligence, diseases like diabetes)
Argue with evidence from chapter and beyond
Conclude with balanced scientific view

activities_projects.title: Mutation Impact Research Project
steps: Research one mutation (sickle cell anemia, Down syndrome)
Explain cause, inheritance pattern, effects
Discuss advantage in heterozygous state (e.g., malaria resistance)
Present as poster/report

activities_projects.title: Advanced Heredity Quiz with Numerical Problems
steps: Prepare/include problems on ratios, probabilities, crosses
Conduct timed quiz in groups
Discuss solutions with explanations

activities_projects.title: Innovative Genetic Solution Idea
steps: Identify issue (e.g., crop disease resistance, genetic disorder prevention)
Propose selective breeding or simple genetic idea
Sketch model, explain inheritance basis
Present feasibility and ethics
